# AgentPack

> Local context engine for AI coding agents.

AgentPack ranks relevant repository files and builds compact task-focused context packs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, MCP tools, CI jobs, and markdown-based LLM workflows.

Use AgentPack when an AI coding agent needs local/offline repo analysis before editing code. It prepares ranked files, likely tests, repo rules, installed skills, suggested commands, freshness metadata, and benchmark diagnostics.

Core links:

- Docs: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/
- GitHub: https://github.com/vishal2612200/agentpack
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/agentpack-cli/
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vishal2612200/agentpack
- How it works: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/how-agentpack-works/
- AI coding agent context packing: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/ai-coding-agent-context/
- Claude Code context engine: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/claude-code-context-engine/
- MCP context engine: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/mcp-context-engine/
- AgentPack vs Repomix: https://vishal2612200.github.io/agentpack/agentpack-vs-repomix/

Install:

```bash
pipx install agentpack-cli
agentpack route --task "fix auth token expiry"
```

npm wrapper:

```bash
npx @vishal2612200/agentpack route --task "fix auth token expiry"
```

Important boundaries:

- AgentPack is not a coding agent.
- AgentPack does not upload repository code for normal scan, rank, pack, stats, or benchmark workflows.
- AgentPack does not require embeddings, hosted indexing, or a vector database for core context packing.
- AgentPack's benchmark results are scoped release evidence, not universal proof of coding-agent success.
